creator: matthew@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is an OpenPGP/MIME signed message (RFC 2440 and 3156)
 --------------enig0C4CA317AD16B5D0589C6892
 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1
 Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable
 
 On 13/10/2012 20:18, Joe Horn wrote:
 
 > -RUN_DEPENDS=3D	p5-DBD-mysql>1.0:${PORTSDIR}/databases/p5-DBD-mysql \
 > +RUN_DEPENDS=3D	p5-DBD-mysql${MYSQL_VER}>=3D0:${PORTSDIR}/databases/p5-=
 DBD-mysql${MYSQL_VER} \
 
 This again.  I'm sorry, but this is a step in the wrong direction.  Just
 because the versioned p5-DBD-mysql ports exist does not make them a good
 idea.  Instead of pushing this complexity into every perl port that
 might need to connect to a mysql database, it's much better just to use
 the generic p5-DBD-mysql compiled against whichever version of mysql you
 prefer.
 
 	Cheers,
 
 	Matthew
 
 --=20
 Dr Matthew J Seaman MA, D.Phil.
 PGP: http://www.infracaninophile.co.uk/pgpkey
